meta.title: Ulta Context Engine Mega Brief
meta.description: Current architecture, schema direction, source readiness, and implementation slices for Ulta Context Engine.

props.title: Context Engine Mega Brief
props.subtitle: Repo-owned named context, prompt catalogs, delivery schemas, and local-first search projections.

props.content: The durable direction is simple: repos own human docs and named `.ulta/context`; prompt catalogs seed the work that produces that context; the context engine owns schemas, graph admission, and projections; the Ulta AI CLI is the product adapter that installs, initializes, configures, and runs the workflows.

props.label: Current Proof
props.title: What is real locally right now

props.label: Boundary
props.title: Current versus ideal model
props.subtitle: This keeps the source of truth in repos while allowing QMD and graph to be rebuilt as projections.

props.title: Current
props.content: A working legacy corpus proof combines web-site docs, curated context, DXL model extraction, and orchestration rules. Repo `.ulta` folders have been initialized, but BE, DXL, iOS, and Android named context are not populated yet.

props.title: Ideal
props.content: Each repo carries a small global named context set plus repo-type extensions. The context engine validates schemas and builds QMD/search/graph projections. Workflow skills query by role, lifecycle stage, artifact type, and stack intent.

props.title: Context Engine Flow
props.content:
flowchart TD
  A["Repo sources\ndocs, code, rules, models"] --> B["Prompt catalogs\nulta-context-* packages"]
  B --> C["Scaffolded prompts\n.ulta/prompts"]
  C --> D["Named context\n.ulta/context"]
  D --> E["Context engine\nvalidators and schemas"]
  E --> F["QMD / local search\nfast local projection"]
  E --> G["Graph entities\nrelationships and receipts"]
  F --> H["Ulta AI CLI query"]
  G --> H
  H --> I["Role skills\nestimation, epic, arch, QA, dev tickets"]
  H --> J["Optional MCP adapter"]

props.title: Discovered items in current proof lanes

rows.purpose: Lifecycle aggregate from intake through delivery receipts
Public work wrapper: deliveryObject, id, name, desc, jiraId, meta
Ordered route from Ingest to Push with repeated QA/proof stages
Retrieval atom with audience, domain, stage, category, group, repo type, artifact targets
Typed outputs like QA report, dev ticket, user story, C1-C4, wiki ref, receipts
Ordered catalog selection that scaffolds prompts producing named context

props.label: CLI UX
props.title: Prompt catalogs produce named context
props.subtitle: These are product steps, not hidden config surgery.

props.title: Install
props.content: `ulta install` prepares the local product surface, skills, and optional adapters.

props.title: Init repo
props.content: User enters a repo and asks for `ulta init`.

props.title: Detect type
props.content: Agent/CLI scans and proposes a repo type, for example node/react.

props.title: Apply config
props.content: `config apply repo-type` and `config apply prompt-catalog` mutate config through product commands.

props.title: Scaffold prompts
props.content: Catalog files materialize under `.ulta/prompts` in declared array order.

props.title: Generate context
props.content: Prompts produce named `.ulta/context` files that can be indexed or promoted to graph.

props.label: Config
props.title: Authored config is prompt catalog selection
Do not revive context overlays
props.content: The selected product shape is ordered prompt catalogs. The prompts are what produce named context. Array index is scaffold, execution, and precedence order.

context:
  prompts:
    directory: prompts
    catalogs:
      - id: repo-core
        version: v1
        mode: seed
        enabled: true
      - id: web-experience
        version: v1
        mode: seed
        enabled: true

# Catalog source files:
# ulta-context-*/prompts/<prompt-catalog>-<prompt-slug>.md
# Scaffolded files:
# .ulta/prompts/<prompt-catalog>-<prompt-slug>.md

props.label: Lifecycle
props.title: Delivery route needs explicit stage IDs

items.title: Ingest
items.description: VP, PO, PM, BA, stakeholder input becomes a DeliveryObject draft.

items.title: Planning
items.description: High-level epic planning, t-shirt estimates, story framing, acceptance criteria, and risks.

items.title: Architecture approval
items.description: C1-C4, ADRs, dependency maps, and boundary decisions mature the work before dev grooming.

items.title: Dev and initial Verify/QA/Proof
items.description: Implementation tickets add repo-specific context; QA starts from the business slice and adds test depth.

items.title: Cleanup, standards, final proof, receipt, push
items.description: Refactor, standards/perf/ADA, final QA proof, commit receipt, and push receipts close the delivery trail.

props.label: Routing
props.title: Artifacts and ContextItems are the working units
ArtifactRecord
props.content: Typed outputs include user stories, dev tickets, QA test reports, C1-C4 architecture artifacts, wiki references, estimate reports, test plans, acceptance traceability, receipts, release notes, and rollback plans.

props.title: ContextItem
props.content: Retrieval atoms carry audience, domain, lifecycle stage keys, category, parent, group, repo types, capabilities, artifact targets, and inclusion policy. This is how business, dev, and QA see the right depth.

props.title: Local-first before graph completeness
props.content: The current proof can generate a markdown corpus with web docs, curated context, DXL models, and backend rules. The next v1 path should query across all repo-owned `.ulta/context` and docs sources, then promote accepted structured entities into the graph.

props.title: Intent expansion policy
props.content: For a request like adding a payment method or promotion rule, the skill should project the completed solution first, then issue stack-wide discovery queries plus stack-specific queries for client surfaces, DXL/BFF, backend services, business rules, and QA artifacts.

rows.current: Repo `.ulta/context` exists but is mostly empty or ad hoc
rows.ideal: Every repo has global named context plus repo-type extensions
rows.next: Define global prompt catalog and run it in each repo

rows.current: Design captured; no canonical package yet
rows.ideal: Context engine exports validated contracts imported by CLI
rows.next: Implement DeliveryObject, UnitOfWork, ContextItem, ArtifactRecord, catalog schemas

rows.current: Seed prompts exist, but not cataloged with ordered selection
rows.ideal: Catalog abstraction shared by skills, repo types, and prompt scaffolding
rows.next: Build catalog discovery, plan, apply, receipts

rows.current: Legacy corpus proof exists; repo-owned `.ulta/context` projection is incomplete
rows.ideal: Cross-repo query over web, DXL, BE, iOS, Android context and docs
rows.next: Build projection from repo sources, then prove stack-wide queries

rows.current: Entity design exists; durable graph is not the source yet
rows.ideal: Accepted entities and relationships promoted with receipts and versioning
rows.next: Create graph admission schema and projection receipts

props.label: Build Plan
props.title: Implementation slices to get to useful queries

items.title: Contracts package
items.description: Context engine exports schemas, validators, route catalogs, artifact taxonomy, and migration helpers.

items.title: Prompt catalog and CLI adapter
items.description: Ordered prompt catalogs become product config; CLI handles repo detection, config apply, scaffold, dry-run, and receipts.

items.title: Global named context generation
items.description: Business context, system context, architecture overview, and delivery/gotchas generated into each repo.

items.title: Repo-type extensions
items.description: Web, DXL, backend, iOS, and Android prompt catalogs produce stack-specific named context.

items.title: Cross-repo QMD proof
items.description: Queries return relevant web, DXL, and backend results for promotion/payment/content scenarios.

items.title: Graph promotion and workflow skills
items.description: Promote accepted entities and wire estimation, epic creation, architecture, dev ticket, and QA skills to the query policy.
